correct these.
You're working for not heavy traffic on the street.
everything else is help to the environment.
that allowed you not to be overwhelmed.

You're working to avoid heavy traffic on the street.
Everything else is helping the environment.
That allowed you to avoid feeling overwhelmed.
is this phrase correct? That allowed you not to be overwhelmed

Yes, the phrase "That allowed you not to be overwhelmed" is grammatically correct. The phrase effectively conveys the meaning that a certain action or situation prevented you from becoming overwhelmed.

they do not know nothing about life.
the kids doesn't have this kind of experiences.
he's not putting attention to everything around him.
this can became similar to playing in the casino.
you are able to access to this kind of things.

Here are the corrected sentences:

It's kind of worrying.
They don't know anything about life.
The kids don't have these kinds of experiences.
He's not paying attention to everything around him.
This can become similar to playing in a casino.
You are able to access these kinds of things.

fix this statement.
Hello there, nice work during another fun and engaging session. I enjoyed talking to you about the topic, because you have so many insights that you're able to share. We also talked about our perspective on gaming in general, and how some games can offer good mental stimuli, however majority of games can cause addiction if not properly controlled or supervised, particularly to children up to young adult generations. In that session you demonstrated very good English as usual. Your grammar is mostly on point, vocabulary is extensive and no problem with pronunciation. I didn't noticed you struggling in any in finding the words you need, and we just went on and on and on until the end of the session. You're doing excellently, keep it up!

Hello there, great job during another fun and engaging session. I enjoyed our conversation about the topic, as you have so many insights to share. We also discussed our perspectives on gaming in general and how some games can provide good mental stimulation. However, a majority of games can lead to addiction if not properly controlled or supervised, particularly for children and young adults. In that session, you demonstrated your usual strong command of English. Your grammar is mostly on point, your vocabulary is extensive, and pronunciation is not a problem. I didn't notice you struggling to find the words you needed, and our conversation flowed smoothly right up to the end of the session. You're doing exceptionally well, keep it up!
what does it mean? I have my flight the other week.

The sentence "I have my flight the other week" is a bit unclear because "the other week" is an imprecise term that typically refers to a week different from the current one, but it doesn't specify whether it's the past or future. In typical English conversation, when referring to the past, one might say "the other week" to denote a week that has already passed, somewhat equivalent to saying "the other day" for a past day. However, in the context of an upcoming flight, the speaker likely means to say "I have my flight next week" or "I have my flight in a week's time," which would indicate a flight scheduled for the following week. It may be useful to seek clarification from the speaker to ensure the correct interpretation.

The word "plane" is pronounced as follows:

- Start with the 'p' sound by pressing your lips together and releasing a burst of air.
- Follow with the 'l' sound by placing the tip of your tongue against the alveolar ridge, just behind your upper front teeth.
- Then add the 'ay' sound, which is similar to the 'a' in "say" or "day." It's a long vowel sound made by opening your mouth and letting your tongue hover in the middle without touching the sides or roof of your mouth.
- Finally, end with the 'n' sound by placing your tongue against the alveolar ridge once more and letting the air pass through your nose.

Phonetically, it is written as /pleɪn/.

So, "plane" rhymes with "rain," "main," and "train."

Hello Jorge. If you have something you need to attend to today and will need to cancel the class, we can either reschedule today's session on a date and time we agreed upon. Or we can skip this and we'll have our regular session as normaly next week.

Hello Jorge. If you have something you need to attend to today and will need to cancel the class, we can reschedule today's session to a date and time we both agree on. Alternatively, we can skip today and have our regular session as normal next week.
